title: oxalis tysonii
intro: Oxalis tysonii, also known as the Tyson's sorrel or Mountain sorrel, is a perennial plant species in the wood sorrel family native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and antiseptic, to treat various health issues such as urinary tract infections and skin conditions. The plant is rich in ascorbic acid and oxalic acid, which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, providing potential health benefits such as improved immune function and reduced inflammation. Oxalis tysonii can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it has also been used in traditional Maori medicine for its purported health benefits.
